title: iris ensata var. pabularia
intro: Iris ensata var. pabularia, also known as the Japanese iris or 'Pabularia' Japanese iris, is a variety of flowering plant in the iris family. Traditionally used in Eastern medicine, it has been valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote sweating and relieve congestion. Iris ensata var. pabularia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of illness.
